summary: Iter13 — Per-user MODULE ACCESS permissions: full backend + frontend testing complete. RESULT: ALL CHECKS PASS. Backend: 24/24 new pytest cases (test_iter13_module_access.py) — modules catalog (admin-only, 403 cashier), POST /users with explicit modules, role-default fallback, admin-role forces ALL 11 modules, bad module names filtered, PATCH role/name/password/modules, PATCH role->admin auto-locks ALL_MODULES, /auth/me + /auth/login expose allowed_modules, module gating on 8 endpoints (dashboard+payments 200, clients/finance/items/contracts/auctions/reports 403), PATCH adds clients -> /clients 200 on same session, admin bypass on 8 endpoints, backfill verified across all DB users (admins=ALL_MODULES, non-admins non-empty). Iter10 regression: 15/15 PASS. Frontend Playwright (1920x1080): Users page shows Modules column with badges (admin row='All modules'), dialog opens with Module Access section + 11 checkboxes + 4 preset buttons; preset-none clears, preset-cashier=dashboard+payments, preset-staff=7 modules, preset-all=11; selecting role=admin auto-locks + disables all checkboxes AND disables all 4 preset buttons; created cashier with cashier preset (dashboard+payments only) -> row appears with correct badges; edit dialog pre-fills email (disabled), pre-checks current modules, toggling 'clients' on persists via PATCH (verified via badge update); login as cashier -> sidebar renders ONLY Dashboard + Payments (2 nav links, /dashboard /payments), all 9 forbidden routes hidden (clients,items,contracts,auctions,reports,finance,users,settings,audit-log); manual nav to /clients yields 403 from API (visible in network) and Clients table shows 'No clients' empty state. Full cleanup: 3 TEST_ cashiers deleted post-test.

issues: No visible toast/banner explaining 403 — Clients page just shows generic 'No clients' empty state. Acceptable per spec ('they can navigate but data is empty/blocked; full route guard can be P2') but a small inline 'You don't have access to the clients module' banner would improve UX.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py: ROLE_DEFAULT_MODULES['admin'] = ALL_MODULES is a list reference, not a copy. If anywhere in code someone mutates ROLE_DEFAULT_MODULES['admin'] or the returned list it would also mutate ALL_MODULES. Currently no code does this (everywhere uses list(ALL_MODULES) when assigning), but consider ROLE_DEFAULT_MODULES['admin'] = list(ALL_MODULES) for safety.

server.py /users/modules endpoint (line 291) is defined AFTER /users/{user_id} PATCH (line 266) and DELETE (line 297) — works because FastAPI matches based on method+path and these are GET-only collisions are avoided. Still, conventional placement would be alongside other GET /users routes (move to ~line 240, before /users/{user_id} variants) to follow least-surprise ordering.

server.py require_module reads user.allowed_modules from the JWT-derived DB lookup in get_current_user (DB hit per request) — this is correct (so PATCH-add-clients takes effect immediately even on existing session), but causes one extra Mongo round-trip per request. For high-traffic scaling consider caching at request scope. Non-blocking.

Users.js (404 lines) is well-structured. MODULE_LABELS hardcoded in Users.js — duplicates module catalog meaning (catalog comes from API, labels from FE). Acceptable for i18n flexibility but consider extracting labels to API response for single-source-of-truth.
Users.js openEdit: form.password reset to '' on edit (line 81). PATCH only sends password if non-empty (line 113). Good — won't clobber existing password.
AdminLayout.js line 70: `user?.allowed_modules || []` — if user object missing the field entirely (shouldn't happen post-backfill, but defensive), non-admin sees empty sidebar (graceful). Good.
Users.js dialog 'Save' button has no loading state — rapid double-click could create duplicate users for non-edit case. Minor UX (backend would 409 on duplicate email anyway).

Frontend /clients (and other module-gated pages) has no client-side route guard — non-admin can navigate URL manually, gets API 403, sees empty table with no explicit message. Spec explicitly says this is acceptable (P2 future work).
test_credentials: admin@fatinpenhores.tl / admin123 (per /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Test cashiers created on-the-fly per test and deleted post-run.
seed_data_creation: Created and deleted ~3 TEST_ cashier users during testing (test_ui_cashier_*, test_sidebar_*, test_sidebar2_*). All cleaned up via final cleanup step that deletes any user with test_sidebar/test_ui_cashier prefix. Backend pytest fixtures use yield+teardown to delete created users automatically. No persistent pollution.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter13 adds per-user module access. Key files: /app/backend/server.py has ALL_MODULES (11 strings), ROLE_DEFAULT_MODULES (admin=all, staff=7, cashier=[dashboard,payments]), require_module(name) factory, GET /api/users/modules (admin-only), backfill on startup. /app/frontend/src/pages/Users.js has Module Access checkbox grid + 4 preset buttons (preset-staff, preset-cashier, preset-all, preset-none) + edit support. /app/frontend/src/layouts/AdminLayout.js filters sidebar by user.allowed_modules. Important: require_module returns immediately for role=admin (admins never gated). Login response is FLAT (not wrapped in 'user' key) — has allowed_modules at top level. Iter13 backend test file: /app/backend/tests/test_iter13_module_access.py (24 tests, 9s runtime). Iter10 regression still 15/15. All test users have prefix test_* and are auto-cleaned via fixture teardown.
rca of the issue: Not applicable — feature verification (no bugs found). All 13 bullet-pointed test cases from the review request pass exactly as specified.
